rules.description: Enforce getter and setter pairs in objects and classes
Enforce return statements in callbacks of array methods
Require braces around arrow function bodies

rules.description: Enforce the use of variables within the scope they are defined
Enforce or disallow capitalization of the first letter of a comment

rules.description: Enforce that class methods utilize this
Enforce a maximum cyclomatic complexity allowed in a program
Require return statements to either always or never specify values
Enforce consistent naming when capturing the current execution context
Enforce consistent brace style for all control statements

Enforce default clauses in switch statements to be last
Enforce default parameters to be last

Require function names to match the name of the variable or property to which they are assigned
Require or disallow named function expressions

Require for-in loops to include an if statement
Enforce minimum and maximum identifier lengths

rules.description: Require identifiers to match a specified regular expression
Require or disallow initialization in variable declarations

rules.exampleGood:

rules.description: Require or disallow logical assignment operator shorthand
Enforce a maximum number of classes per file

123

rules.description: Enforce a maximum depth that blocks can be nested

Enforce a maximum number of lines of code in a function

rules.description: Enforce a maximum depth that callbacks can be nested
Enforce a maximum number of parameters in function definitions
Enforce a maximum number of statements allowed in function blocks

1

rules.description: Disallow the use of alert , confirm , and prompt

rules.description: Disallow using an async function as a Promise executor

rules.exampleBad:

1

rules.description: Disallow the use of arguments.caller or arguments.callee
Disallow lexical declarations in case clauses
Disallow assignment operators in conditional expressions
Disallow reassigning const , using , and await using variables
Disallow expressions where the operation doesn’t affect the value
Disallow control characters in regular expressions
Disallow equal signs explicitly at the beginning of regular expressions
rules.exampleBad:
function bar() { return /=foo/; }
1
rules.exampleGood:
function bar() { return /=foo/; }
123

rules.description: Disallow duplicate arguments in function definitions
Disallow duplicate conditions in if-else-if chains
Disallow duplicate keys in object literals
Disallow else blocks after return statements in if statements
Disallow empty character classes in regular expressions
rules.exampleBad:
const foo = /^abc[]/;
1
rules.exampleGood:
/^abc[]/.test("abcdefg"); // false
"abcdefg".match(/^abc[]/); // null

/^abc[[]]/v.test("abcdefg"); // false
"abcdefg".match(/^abc[[]]/v); // null

/^abc[[]--[x]]/v.test("abcdefg"); // false
"abcdefg".match(/^abc[[]--[x]]/v); // null

/^abc[[d]&&[]]/v.test("abcdefg"); // false
"abcdefg".match(/^abc[[d]&&[]]/v); // null

const regex = /^abc[d[]]/v;
regex.test("abcdefg"); // true, the nested `[]` has no effect
"abcdefg".match(regex); // ["abcd"]
regex.test("abcefg"); // false, the nested `[]` has no effect
"abcefg".match(regex); // null
regex.test("abc"); // false, the nested `[]` has no effect
"abc".match(regex); // null
123456789101112131415161718192021

rules.exampleBad:
var foo = /^abc[]/;
1

rules.exampleGood:
list.map(() => {});   // This is a block, would return undefined.
list.map(() => ({})); // This is an empty object.
12

rules.description: Disallow null comparisons without type-checking operators
Disallow reassigning exceptions in catch clauses

rules.exampleBad:
<!--
// this is a script
// -->

("use strict");

function test() {
    console.log(typeof this);
}

test();
1234567891011

rules.description: Disallow assignments to native objects or read-only global variables
rules.exampleBad:
window = {};
1
rules.exampleGood:
Object = null
undefined = 1
1234

rules.description: Disallow declarations in the global scope
Disallow the use of eval() -like methods
rules.exampleBad:
setTimeout("alert('Hi!');", 100);
1

import mod, { named } from "./mod.mjs"
import * as mod_ns from "./mod.mjs"

mod = 1          // ERROR: 'mod' is readonly.
named = 2        // ERROR: 'named' is readonly.
mod_ns.named = 3 // ERROR: The members of 'mod_ns' are readonly.
mod_ns = {}      // ERROR: 'mod_ns' is readonly.
// Can't extend 'mod_ns'
Object.assign(mod_ns, { foo: "foo" }) // ERROR: The members of 'mod_ns' are readonly.
1234567891011
rules.exampleGood:
import mod, { named } from "./mod.mjs"
import * as mod_ns from "./mod.mjs"

mod.prop = 1
named.prop = 2
mod_ns.named.prop = 3

// Known Limitation
function test(obj) {
    obj.named = 4 // Not errored because 'obj' is not namespace objects.
}
test(mod_ns) // Not errored because it doesn't know that 'test' updates the member of the argument.
1234567891011121314

\u0085 - Next Line - <NEL>
\u00A0 - No-Break Space - <NBSP>

\u2001 - Em Quad - <MQSP>
\u2002 - En Space - <ENSP>

\u2007 - Figure Space - <FSP>

\u2009 - Thin Space - <THSP>
\u200A - Hair Space - <HSP>
\u200B - Zero Width Space - <ZWSP>
\u2028 - Line Separator - <LS> - <LSEP>

rules.description: Disallow the use of the __iterator__ property
Disallow labels that share a name with a variable
Disallow if statements as the only statement in else blocks
Disallow function declarations that contain unsafe references inside loop statements
Disallow literal numbers that lose precision
Disallow characters which are made with multiple code points in character class syntax

rules.exampleBad:
var foo = a && b || c || d;    /*BAD: Unexpected mix of '&&' and '||'.*/
var foo = (a && b) || c || d;  /*GOOD*/
var foo = a && (b || c || d);  /*GOOD*/
123
rules.exampleGood:
var foo = a && b || c || d;
1

rules.exampleBad:
var fs = require('fs'),        // "core"     \
    async = require('async'),  // "module"   |- these are "require declaration"s

    bar = require(getName()),  // "computed" /
    baz = 42,                  // "other"
    bam;                       // "uninitialized"
123456

Disallow calling global object properties as functions

rules.description: Disallow ternary operators when simpler alternatives exist
Disallow unreachable code after return , throw , continue , and break statements
Disallow loops with a body that allows only one iteration
Disallow control flow statements in finally blocks
Disallow negating the left operand of relational operators
Disallow use of optional chaining in contexts where the undefined value is not allowed
rules.exampleBad:

1 in obj?.foo;  // TypeError
with (obj?.foo);  // TypeError
for (bar of obj?.foo);  // TypeError
bar instanceof obj?.foo;  // TypeError
const { bar } = obj?.foo;  // TypeError

rules.exampleGood:

(obj?.foo)(); // TypeError
(obj?.foo).bar; // TypeError

rules.description: Disallow the use of variables before they are defined

Disallow useless backreferences in regular expressions
rules.exampleBad:

regex.test("a"); // true
regex.test("b"); // true!

const equivalentRegex = /^(?:(a)|b)$/;

equivalentRegex.test("a"); // true
equivalentRegex.test("b"); // true
equivalentRegex.test("ab"); // false

rules.description: Require or disallow assignment operator shorthand where possible
rules.exampleBad:
Shorthand | Separate
-----------|------------
 x += y    | x = x + y
 x -= y    | x = x - y
 x *= y    | x = x * y
 x /= y    | x = x / y
 x %= y    | x = x % y
 x **= y   | x = x ** y
 x <<= y   | x = x << y
 x >>= y   | x = x >> y
 x >>>= y  | x = x >>> y
 x &= y    | x = x & y
 x ^= y    | x = x ^ y
 x |= y    | x = x | y
1234567891011121314

rules.description: Require using arrow functions for callbacks
Require const declarations for variables that are never reassigned after declared
Require destructuring from arrays and/or objects
Disallow the use of Math.pow in favor of the ** operator
Enforce using named capture group in regular expression

rules.description: Disallow parseInt() and Number.parseInt() in favor of binary, octal, and hexadecimal literals
Disallow use of Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call() and prefer use of Object.hasOwn()
Disallow using Object.assign with an object literal as the first argument and prefer the use of object spread instead
Require using Error objects as Promise rejection reasons

Require rest parameters instead of arguments
Require template literals instead of string concatenation

Enforce the use of the radix argument when using parseInt()

rules.exampleGood:

Disallow async functions which have no await expression
Enforce the use of u or v flag on regular expressions
Require generator functions to contain yield
